title: Dense Malaysian Vegetable Fried Rice
description: A hearty and flavorful Malaysian dish made with stir-fry vegetables and dense rice, perfect for a hangover cure or a satisfying meal. This recipe is egg-free and friendly for various dietary needs.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the grated carrots and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the green beans and shredded cabbage, and cook until the vegetables are tender-crisp, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the cooked rice to the wok or frying pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir-fry the rice with the vegetables for about 5 minutes, until everything is well combined and the rice is heated through.
Add the soy sauce and oyster sauce (if using), and stir-fry for another minute.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the fried rice to a serving platter and serve immediately.
